div.h2: Module Architecture: sha256_padded_t and Public Interface

div.p: data[] uint8_t data[SHA256_MAX_PADDED_BLOCKS * SHA256_BLOCK_SIZE] flat byte buffer — caller-allocated, no heap layout: [msg bytes][0x80][0x00 fill][64-bit BE length] valid range: data[0 .. num_blocks*64 - 1]
num_blocks size_t num_blocks count of 64-byte blocks in data[] range: [1, SHA256_MAX_PADDED_BLOCKS] set by sha256_pad(); read by compression loop

ol.li: bytes between marker and length field are 0x00

div.p: Signature int sha256_pad( const uint8_t *msg, size_t len, sha256_padded_t *out) Returns 0 = success · -1 = len > MAX Parameters msg — caller owns; read-only; NULL iff len==0 len — message length in bytes; [0, SHA256_MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES] out — caller allocates; callee fills; must not be NULL

Signature const uint8_t *sha256_get_block( const sha256_padded_t *padded, size_t block_index) Returns pointer to padded->data[block_index × 64] pointer valid until padded goes out of scope Precondition block_index < padded->num_blocks (caller must bounds-check; no runtime guard) Implementation return padded->data + (block_index * SHA256_BLOCK_SIZE); single pointer addition — O(1), no copy

Responsibilities • allocate sha256_padded_t (stack or heap) • provide valid msg pointer (or NULL for empty) • check return code of sha256_pad() • bounds-check block_index before sha256_get_block() • iterate i = 0 .. num_blocks-1 in compression loop
receives const uint8_t *block (64 bytes) from sha256_get_block() return value reads bytes as big-endian 32-bit words W[0..15] then expands to W[16..63] via σ0/σ1 recurrence

Solid arrow → writes / fills (callee responsibility) Dashed arrow → reads / borrows (read-only access) Yellow boxes → public API functions Blue box → primary data structure (caller-owned) Green box → internal helper (static linkage) Purple box → constants (#define, compile-time)
